# abapctl

> abapctl is a standalone CLI for SAP ABAP Development Tools (ADT) and the OData runtime. It gives terminals, scripts, and AI agents direct, headless, JSON-first access to a live SAP system over HTTPS: read and navigate ABAP, write it through the lock/write/unlock/activate cycle, run ATC and ABAP Unit checks, manage transports, assess and remediate toward Clean Core, and query published OData services. It is a deterministic primitive; an AI agent supplies the reasoning on top.

Notes for readers:
- For the entire documentation in one file, see https://abapctl.github.io/llms-full.txt.
- abapctl is distributed as open binaries (not open source) under MIT-0. Get it from the GitHub releases repo below.
- Every command reports three safety booleans in `tools list --json` (`readOnly`, `destructive`, `idempotent`), supports `--json`, and previews writes with `--dry-run`.
- Works with any ADT-enabled SAP system (ECC or S/4HANA). Some capabilities depend on the backend release.

## Getting started

- [Introduction](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/): what abapctl is and how it reaches SAP (ADT design-time and OData runtime).
- [Overview](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/getting-started/overview): the two-layer model (deterministic primitive plus an agent that reasons) and who it is for.
- [Install](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/getting-started/install): download the binary for Linux, macOS, or Windows.
- [Configure](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/getting-started/configure): connections, ports, authentication, and password sources.
- [Quickstart](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/getting-started/quickstart): first commands against a system.

## Core concepts

- [Architecture](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/concepts/architecture): the CLI, workflow, capability, and SAP-client layers.
- [Connections & sessions](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/concepts/connections-sessions): profiles, precedence, and session reuse.
- [Lock, write, activate](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/concepts/lock-write-activate): the write lifecycle and the lock-finally guard.
- [Capabilities & gating](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/concepts/capabilities): how abapctl adapts to what a backend supports.
- [JSON & safety](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/concepts/json-and-safety): the output contract, safety flags, exit codes, and error codes.

## Guides

- [Read and navigate](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/read-navigate): source download, search, definitions, references, completion.
- [Edit source](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/edit-source): the lock/write/unlock/activate cycle and batch activation.
- [Quality checks](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/quality-checks): syntax, ATC, ABAP Unit, CDS-DDL, and the ATC quick-fix loop.
- [Transports](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/transports): create, inspect, release, and delete transport requests.
- [Workspaces](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/workspaces): sync a package to local files, like git clone for SAP.
- [Object creation](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/object-creation): the create command family and `--source` inputs.
- [DDIC and CDS](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/ddic-cds): DDIC deep metadata, CDS intelligence, and data preview.
- [Services and OData](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/services-odata): service bindings and consuming OData runtime services.
- [Behavior definitions and RAP](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/bdef-rap): BDEF read/create, batch activation, and ENHO.
- [Clean Core](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/clean-core): the assess, prep, fix, apply remediation loop.
- [Working with AI agents](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/guides/ai-agents): the `tools list --json` contract and the propose/approve/commit loop.

## Command reference

- [Command reference](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/command-reference): every command grouped by family, with safety badges (READ, IDEMP, DESTR, WRITE) generated from `tools list --json`.

## Skills and agents

- [Skills & Agents](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/skills-agents): how agents drive abapctl and how to install skills.
- [The sap-abap skill](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/skills-agents/sap-abap-skill): the decision-aid skill for driving abapctl.
- [Clean Core skills](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/skills-agents/clean-core-skills): the four skills and the remediator subagent.

## Agentic use cases

- [Agentic use cases](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/use-cases): three worked examples of agent-driven SAP work.
- [S/4HANA readiness and quick fixes](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/use-cases/readiness): find blockers and apply SAP's deterministic quick fixes.
- [Clean Core assessment and remediation](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/use-cases/clean-core): assess a package and remediate with the agent.
- [Test-driven development](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/use-cases/tdd): build new ABAP test-first with the agent.
- [Going further](https://abapctl.github.io/docs/use-cases/going-further): more agentic workflows to adapt.

## Source and releases

- [Binaries and releases](https://github.com/aws-for-sap/Automate-SAP-development-workflows-using-ABAP-CTL): download abapctl (open distribution, MIT-0).
- [Clean Core kit and skills](https://github.com/aws-for-sap/agentic-ai-guidance-for-SAP-use-cases/tree/main/clean-core): the sap-abap and clean-core skills plus the remediator subagent.
